facts.label: Tech fact of the day
facts.body: New AI‑driven ransomware can autonomously locate vulnerable databases, harvest credentials, and encrypt data without any human intervention. This end‑to‑end automation shows how generative AI models can be weaponized, underscoring the need for AI‑aware threat detection, strict network segmentation, and multi‑factor authentication to protect critical data stores.
facts.sourceTitle: 'No hacker needed': New AI ransomware can automate database attacks from start to finish, warns cybersecu - The Times of India

facts.label: Tech fact of the day
facts.body: Cooling hardware is vital in semiconductor fabrication because lithography and etching steps generate tens of kilowatts of heat that can warp wafers and increase defect rates. Advanced chillers, liquid CO₂ cooling loops, and real‑time thermal monitoring keep process temperatures sub‑ambient, preserving device geometry and yield.
facts.sourceTitle: Why cooling hardware plays a critical role in semiconductor fabrication - Engineer Live

facts.label: Tech fact of the day
facts.body: FatFs is a popular open-source file system library used in many IoT and embedded devices, but it has been found to contain seven bugs that put these devices at risk of attack. These bugs can be exploited by attackers to gain unauthorized access to the device, steal sensitive data, or disrupt its operation. Developers who use FatFs in their projects should patch these bugs as soon as possible to ensure the security and reliability of their devices.
facts.sourceTitle: Seven Bugs in FatFs Put IoT and Embedded Devices at Risk - Security Affairs

facts.label: Tech fact of the day
facts.body: Illinois Democrats are weighing the pros and cons of using AI, recognizing both the benefits and risks associated with this technology. As AI becomes more prevalent in various industries, it is essential to consider the potential consequences of its adoption, including job displacement and bias. By engaging in informed discussions about AI, we can ensure that its development and deployment align with human values.
facts.sourceTitle: Illinois Democrats weigh pros and cons of using AI — 'We must remain the master of the technology' - Chicago Sun-Times

facts.label: Tech fact of the day
facts.body: Former Founders Fund VC Sam Blond has launched an AI sales start-up, aiming to disrupt the traditional sales landscape with AI-powered tools and platforms. This initiative highlights the potential of AI to transform the sales industry, enabling more efficient and personalized customer interactions. By leveraging AI in sales, companies can improve their customer engagement and revenue growth.
facts.sourceTitle: Former Founders Fund VC Sam Blond launches AI sales startup to upend Salesforce - TechCrunch

facts.label: Tech fact of the day
facts.body: MLB's AI strike zone system has undergone seven years of testing, exposing hidden trade-offs between accuracy and fairness. This experience highlights the challenges of developing and deploying AI systems in complex, real-world environments. By acknowledging these trade-offs, developers can design more effective and fair AI systems.
facts.sourceTitle: Seven years of testing exposed hidden trade-offs in MLB's AI strike zone - Tech Xplore

facts.label: Tech fact of the day
facts.body: Johnson Controls' Metasys 16.0 update focuses on reducing downtime and easing compliance in building management systems, demonstrating the importance of efficient and secure systems in the facilities management industry. This update showcases the role of technology in improving building operations and reducing costs. By streamlining compliance and minimizing downtime, Metasys 16.0 helps facilities managers optimize their systems.
facts.sourceTitle: Johnson Controls’ Metasys 16.0 update aims to reduce downtime, ease compliance - Facilities Dive

facts.label: Tech fact of the day
facts.body: TYREX is bringing its proven USB cybersecurity technology to the US industrial and critical infrastructure sectors, addressing the need for robust security solutions in these high-stakes environments. This technology is designed to protect against cyber threats and ensure the integrity of critical systems. By implementing advanced cybersecurity measures, organizations can safeguard their operations and prevent potential disruptions.
facts.sourceTitle: TYREX Brings Proven USB Cybersecurity Technology to U.S. Industrial and Critical Infrastructure - PRWeb

facts.label: Tech fact of the day
facts.body: The use of AI in cybersecurity is becoming increasingly important, as companies seek to stay ahead of AI-driven threats and protect their systems from sophisticated attacks. By harnessing AI-powered security solutions, organizations can enhance their defenses and respond more effectively to emerging threats. This shift towards AI-driven cybersecurity highlights the evolving nature of the threat landscape and the need for innovative security strategies.
facts.sourceTitle: Harnessing AI in cybersecurity: Ways companies can stay ahead of AI-driven threats - TNGlobal

facts.label: Tech fact of the day
facts.body: Generative AI is changing the way we work with databases, enabling new applications and use cases that were previously impossible or impractical. This technology has the potential to revolutionize data management, making it more efficient and autonomous. By harnessing generative AI, organizations can unlock new insights and drive innovation in various fields, from healthcare to finance.
facts.sourceTitle: How Generative AI Is Changing the Way We Work With Databases - Built In

facts.label: Tech fact of the day
facts.body: Avalue Technology is expanding its Torizon integration to help OEMs meet Cyber Resilience Act requirements, highlighting the importance of robust security measures in industrial systems. This development underscores the need for organizations to prioritize cybersecurity and ensure compliance with emerging regulations. By implementing secure-by-design principles, OEMs can safeguard their products and protect against potential threats.
facts.sourceTitle: Avalue Technology expands Torizon integration to help OEMs meet Cyber Resilience Act requirements - Industrial Cyber

facts.label: Tech fact of the day
facts.body: The development of new database systems and SQL tools is crucial for supporting modern data engineering applications, which require scalable, secure, and efficient data management. Researchers and developers are creating innovative solutions to address these needs, driving progress in fields like data science and machine learning. By advancing database technology, we can enable new applications and use cases that transform industries and improve lives.
facts.sourceTitle: 7 Best GitHub Repositories for Modern Database Systems, SQL Tools, and Data Engineering - Tech Times

facts.label: Tech fact of the day
facts.body: Oracle's MySQL Autopilot Indexing is a significant innovation in database management, as it enables automatic index creation and optimization, reducing the need for manual intervention and expertise. This technology has the potential to improve database performance, simplify maintenance, and enhance overall efficiency. By automating index management, developers can focus on higher-level tasks and drive more value from their applications.
facts.sourceTitle: Removing the index guesstimate with MySQL Autopilot Indexing - Oracle Blogs

facts.label: Tech fact of the day
facts.body: PHP frameworks are essential for building robust, scalable, and secure web applications, and the choice of framework can significantly impact the success of a project. By selecting the most suitable PHP framework, developers can streamline their workflow, improve performance, and reduce the complexity of their applications. This trend highlights the importance of evaluating and choosing the best tools and technologies for specific projects and use cases.
facts.sourceTitle: Top 10 PHP Frameworks: Complete Guide and Future Predictions - appinventiv.com

facts.label: Tech fact of the day
facts.body: Carnegie Mellon University's INI alum Vikramraj Sitpal is advancing database systems at Oracle, demonstrating the significant contributions that researchers and developers can make to the field of database technology. This work highlights the importance of ongoing innovation and research in database systems, which underpin many critical applications and services. By advancing database technology, we can enable new applications and use cases that transform industries and improve lives.
facts.sourceTitle: INI Alum Vikramraj Sitpal Advances Database Systems at Oracle - Information Networking Institute - College of Engineering - Carnegie Mellon University

facts.label: Tech fact of the day
facts.body: The debate around open-source software and AI has sparked intense discussions, with some arguing that the principles of open-source are being threatened by the growing use of AI. The issue revolves around the idea that AI models, which are often trained on open-source data, can be used to create proprietary products that undermine the open-source ethos. This highlights the need for a more nuanced understanding of the relationship between open-source and AI.
facts.sourceTitle: I argued with the father of open source for 2 years. Now the AI fight is the same — only bigger - Fortune

facts.label: Tech fact of the day
facts.body: Getting an open-source product to gain traction can be challenging, requiring a combination of factors such as a clear value proposition, effective community engagement, and strategic marketing. Developers can increase their chances of success by focusing on solving real-world problems, building a strong community around their project, and leveraging social media and other channels to promote their work. By doing so, they can create a loyal user base and drive adoption of their product.
facts.sourceTitle: Ask HN: How do you get your open-source product good traction?

facts.label: Tech fact of the day
facts.body: The Linux Foundation's launch of Akrites, an initiative to defend critical open-source software against AI-enabled cyber threats, is a significant step towards enhancing the security and trustworthiness of open-source components. By working together with industry leaders, the Linux Foundation can help identify and mitigate potential vulnerabilities, ensuring that open-source software is reliable and secure. This initiative highlights the importance of collaboration in the open-source community.
facts.sourceTitle: Linux Foundation and Industry Leaders Launch Akrites to Defend Critical Open Source Software Against AI-Enabled Cyber Threats - Linux Foundation

fact.label: Tech fact of the day
fact.body: Rust's async runtime ecosystem now standardizes on the `async fn` in traits feature stabilized in 1.75, letting embedded and systems programmers write async drivers without boxing every future. For firmware-adjacent services, this means lower allocation pressure on constrained hardware.

fact.label: Tech fact of the day
fact.body: WebGPU is shipping in all major browsers, giving web apps direct GPU compute shaders without WebGL's graphics-only limitations. Computer-vision demos that previously needed native OpenCV can now run inference pipelines in the browser with near-native throughput on integrated GPUs.

fact.label: Tech fact of the day
fact.body: AWS Lambda now supports up to 10 GB memory and 6 vCPU proportionally, letting single-function ETL jobs process larger payloads without splitting across Step Functions. For BFF services that burst on telemetry spikes, right-sizing memory also increases CPU, cutting cold-start decode time.
fact.sourceTitle: AWS Lambda memory and compute configuration

fact.label: Tech fact of the day
fact.body: Playwright's trace viewer records DOM snapshots, network, and console logs per test step—making flaky CI failures reproducible without re-running the full suite locally. Teams rebuilding E2E coverage often cut mean-time-to-diagnose by attaching traces as CI artifacts on first failure only.

fact.label: Tech fact of the day
fact.body: MediaPipe Tasks Vision runs face landmark models entirely in WASM with GPU delegation, hitting 30+ fps on mid-range laptops. That's why browser-based gesture pipelines can match embedded OpenCV latency without a native plugin or server round-trip.

fact.label: Tech fact of the day
fact.body: MongoDB Atlas Search combines Lucene full-text with vector embeddings in one index, so geolocation-filtered delivery apps can run hybrid retrieval without standing up a separate vector database. Fewer moving parts means simpler failover for startup-scale deployments.

fact.label: Tech fact of the day
fact.body: systemd's socket activation lets embedded Linux services start on first connection rather than at boot, shaving seconds off radio-equipment startup sequences on single-core ARM SoCs. Pairing it with watchdog timers gives you automatic restart without custom init scripts.

fact.label: Tech fact of the day
fact.body: Next.js partial prerendering (PPR) streams a static shell instantly while dynamic Redux-driven panels hydrate in parallel—ideal for command-and-control dashboards where map tiles are cacheable but telemetry is live. Operators see layout immediately instead of a blank loading screen.

fact.label: Tech fact of the day
fact.body: D3.js force simulations can offload layout ticks to Web Workers, keeping Leaflet pan/zoom at 60 fps while hundreds of telemetry nodes settle into clusters. Geospatial C2 interfaces use this pattern to decouple data-ingest cadence from render-thread jank.
